title: Harden reservation contention and cleanup
summary: Prove ReservationLedger behavior remains atomic, structured and leak-free under M2 multi-actor contention and terminal cleanup paths.
acceptance: Multi-target claims remain all-or-nothing under entity cell item quantity interaction spot and capacity contention
Cancel fail complete destroy despawn and load-rebuild paths release or rebuild claims without leaks
Lease expiry remains diagnostic/recovery metadata and not normal control flow
Structured reasons distinguish conflict stale target invalid owner insufficient amount insufficient capacity and invalid parameter cases
Contention benchmark finishes with zero unexpected active claims and stable checksums

history.details: Created by WM-0032 M2 planning DAG; remains proposed until dependencies are done.

objective: Make reservation contention safe before larger storage/build jobs depend on it.
forbiddenPaths: lease expiry as normal job progression
benchmarkImpact: Extends reservation artifacts with M2 contention attempts, accepted/rejected counts, conflict classes, cleanup releases and final active claims.
reviewRouting: Owner completes to reviewer; systems-architect must be consulted before owner-store or save-shape changes.
rollbackModel: Revert task branch and benchmark additions. Do not weaken atomicity, fail-closed restore or leak checks.
ownerGate: Prove terminal cleanup for every path touched
Keep ReservationLedger as authoritative claim owner
Run every required check before taskctl complete

sparkEligibility.reason: Reservation contention is concurrency ownership and deterministic cleanup work.
